#  Search for and Filter RFIs 
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Use the search tool to further narrow the displayed results. The search tool respects any selected filter parameters and will only search for items within the filtered results.
    * The filters that you apply (by selecting items in the Add a Filter drop-down list) are persistent and will be retained for your next session, unless you click **Clear All**.


##  Steps 
  * Search for an RFI
  * Filter the RFIs List
  * Sort the RFIs Log


### Search for an RFI
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Click the **Items** tab.
  3. Enter a word or phrase in the **Search** box and press ENTER on your keyboard or click the ![Icon Search](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/0e1287800ad9e8908deec3df3b65ebd0/c5fc5af3e51b77953edf62739fba7b2c/icon-search.png?fm=webp&h=15)icon. 
The search feature supports the use of advanced search symbols. To learn more, see [What is an advanced search symbol in Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-advanced-search-symbol-in-procore)
![Search Rfis](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/544fa2cf02d102ff4384edcde1f4c6e9/310eb37b71e03adaaca87885b8f67bd9/search-rfis.png?fm=webp)
  4. The following fields are searchable in the RFIs tool:
     * Number
     * Subject
     * Question
     * Response


### Filter the RFIs List
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Click the **Items** tab.
  3. Click the **filter**![Icon Filter2](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/1e4b3ef496246c619285ebd3477db671/a7c3fd9c50cac8afbb81c1e492657fb8/icon-filter2.png?fm=webp&h=17) icon and choose your filters.  

##### In Beta
This feature is currently in beta for Procore customers. See [User Guide: Procore Connect for RFIs](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/user-guide-procore-connect-for-RFIs/). 
When projects are connected using the [Connection Manager](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/connection-manager-project/) tool, two more options will appear to identify RFIs linked to External RFIs: 
     * A filterable column titled **Linked To**.
     * A checkbox filter titled **Linked to External RFIs**.
  4. Press ESC on your keyboard to view the filtered list of RFIs.
  5. _Optional:_ Repeat the steps above to add more filters.


### Sort the RFIs Log
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Click the **Items** tab.
  3. Click one of the following column names to sort the list of RFIs by data from the corresponding fields.
  4. _Optional:_ Click the column name again to switch the results between ascending and descending order.


## See Also
  * [RFIs - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * [Edit an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/edit-an-rfi)
  * [What is an advanced search symbol in Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-advanced-search-symbol-in-procore)
title: Search for and Filter RFIs
description: How to find an RFI using the filter and sort options.
